body.title: [dependency] Migrate all dev-process artifacts onto devlab/runtime/memory/; prove the sweep clean
body.description:
**Problem:** Once the inventory (T-substrate-artifact-inventory) lists artifacts living outside devlab/runtime/memory/, they must be reconciled onto the one canonical substrate so tools and skills operate on the same ground. Until then, grep-based readers and the write-path are split.
**Proposed shape:** For each path on the inventory worklist: migrate into the correct devlab/runtime/memory/ subdir (decisions/tickets/slates/sessions/rules/proofs/design_patterns/notes/projects/architecture) via the canonical writer (memory_emit.py / the relevant *_store), VERIFY the migrated copy, then QUARANTINE the source (move to a holding dir, NOT hard-delete) until the end-of-ticket clean-sweep proof passes — only then un-quarantine (delete). **ROLLBACK (HIGH-inertia, non-negotiable):** do the whole migration as ONE git commit so `git revert` restores it, and the quarantine (not hard-delete) means a wrong move is recoverable until the sweep proves clean. Quarantine resolves the tension with the 'no shadow duplicates' lesson — it is temporary (deleted once proven), not a permanent shadow. **SPLIT option (flagged to Akien, not forced):** this can split into (a) migrate+verify+quarantine and (b) delete-quarantine gated on (a)'s clean sweep — decide at sprint time. Honor the inventory's exempt-by-default set (uu_home() runtime state / ~/.claude harness store / ~/.unseen_university/akien* / .git / .TheIgors/certs / credential-shaped files) — record each exemption, do not move blind. Re-run the inventory sweep at the end to prove it returns empty outside the canonical store + recorded exemptions.
**Affected files:** devlab/runtime/memory/** (destination); various source paths under ~/ (per inventory). HIGH-inertia — touches the memory substrate broadly; requires Akien pre-approval before the migration/delete step.
**Design rules:** One home no exceptions; memory_emit.py is the write chokepoint; migrate->VERIFY->delete (no shadow dups); preserve distinct records, delete only confirmed dups; check with Akien before touching .TheIgors/certs or any ~/.unseen_university path.
**Scope boundary:** Only artifacts on the inventory worklist. Each move: verify-then-delete-source. Exemptions recorded, not silently skipped.
**Test plan:** After migration, the Akien sweep — (slate|ticket|goal|session|project|proof|rule)x(md|json|txt) under ~/ minus the exempt-by-default set (uu_home() / ~/.claude / ~/.unseen_university/akien* / .git / .TheIgors/certs / credentials) — returns empty outside devlab/runtime/memory/. Akien's stated acceptance: 'I can't find any dev artifacts anymore except in devlab/runtime/memory/.' Spot-check a migrated record is readable via its canonical store.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
